title: Umami Flaky Potato Crumble
description: This French-inspired dish is a perfect solo-dinner option featuring a flaky potato crumble topped with a creamy chive dip, all slow-cooked to perfection for an indulgent yet sugar-free meal.
instructions: Halve the potatoes lengthwise and scoop out some of the flesh, leaving a half-inch thick wall.
Rub olive oil into the hollowed-out potato halves and sprinkle with salt. Place cut-side down on a baking sheet.
Bake for 45 minutes or until tender. Remove from oven and let cool slightly.
In a large bowl, mix together sour cream, butter, lemon juice, minced garlic, and remaining salt. Mix well.
Gently stuff the potato hollows with the creamy mixture. Return to baking sheet and bake for an additional 15 minutes or until golden brown on top.
Serve hot as is or with a side of fresh chives if desired.
